---
title: "Administration & Configuration"
---

## Administration Overview

When using the Hyland Experience Capture application, documents are scanned or swept in batches into configurable Content Queues. A document in a Content Queue is classified as one of the document classes associated with the Content Queue. Then, the document is indexed using the fields associated with its document class.

In the Hyland Experience Capture Administration interface, you can create and edit fields, document classes, bar code separation profiles, Content Queues, and lists.

-
For information on opening the Administration Interface, see Opening the Administration Interface.

-
For information on configuring fields, see Configuring Fields.

-
For information on configuring document classes, see Configuring Document Classes.

-
For information on configuring bar code separation profiles, see Configuring Bar Code Profiles.

-
For information on configuring Content Queues, see Configuring Content Queues.

-
For information on configuring lists, see Configuring Lists.

## Chrome Restricts Desktop Host

Issue: While using some versions of Chrome to establish a connection between Desktop Host and web applications configured to run on the HTTP protocol, the connection may not be established.

Solution: To prevent cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attacks, Chrome has introduced some restrictions on versions 94 and above. Thus, requests to access private networks from insecure public websites are now deprecated. You can configure Chrome to access restricted private networks from insecure public networks by changing the block-insecure-private-network-requests preference. Alternately, contact your system administrator to have your web applications configured to use only the HTTPS protocol.

For additional information, consult documentation from Chrome on private network access (PNA).

To configure Chrome to access restricted private networks from insecure public networks:

1.  Open a new tab in Chrome.
2.  In the address bar, enter chrome://flags/#block-insecure-private-network-requests.
3.  From the drop-down next to the Block insecure private network request preference, select Disabled.

## Configuring Bar Code Profiles

A bar code profile allows you to configure document separation based on bar codes identified during scanning. With bar code profiles, you can define different separation actions for different bar code types. You can also specify the location of the bar code in the document, and this specified area is enclosed within a nudgeable box. All bar codes detected within the nudgeable box, including bar codes which are partially inside the box, are used in the separation process. If only a part of the bar code is visible on the page, it is not included in the separation process.

-
For a list of all supported bar code types, see Supported Bar Code Types.

-
For information on creating new bar code separation profiles, see Creating a Bar Code Profile.

-
For information on editing existing bar code separation profiles, see Editing a Bar Code Profile.

## Configuring Content Queues

Documents are scanned or swept in batches into configurable Content Queues. A document in a Content Queue can be classified as one of the document classes associated with the Content Queue, and fields associated with the document class can be indexed. The Content Queue can be configured so that indexing either occurs immediately after scanning and import or during verification.

You configure the document classes and classification method associated with a Content Queue when you create or edit the Content Queue.

-
For information on creating new Content Queues, see Creating Content Queues.

-
For information on editing existing Content Queues, see Editing Content Queues.

## Configuring Desktop Host For Identity Providers

This section provides information on how to configure Desktop Host to work with an Identity Provider (IdP) service, such as the Hyland Experience IdP.

Note:
You need to make the following configuration changes to Desktop Host only if it utilizes services that need to be authenticated using an IdP.

Desktop Host requests an access token from the Hyland Experience IdP server. This token is used by services using Desktop Host to authenticate users and allow access to protected resources or actions.

To configure Desktop Host to use an IdP service:

1.  Open the config.json file in a plain-text editor. In a typical installation, this file is in one of the following locations:
-
Before installation, the file is in the same directory as the installer.

-
After Windows installation, the file is in C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost\bin.

-
After macOS installation, the file is in /Library/Hyland/DesktopHost/bin.

2.  Locate the end of the WhitelistedAddress section and insert the following text after it to create a new IdP section:
```
"IdP": {
    "IdPConfigurations": [
        {
            "ServiceName": "IdP Service Name",
            "Issuer": "https://my.domain/identityprovider",
            "ClientID": "Client ID",
            "Scope": "openid offline_access"
        }
    ],
    "IdPServerTimeout":300
}
```

Note:
Each key and value must be in double quotation marks, and pairs of key and value elements must be separated with commas.

3.  For the key "ServiceName", edit the value to specify the name of the IdP service. For example, IdP Service Name.
4.  For the key "Issuer", edit the value to specify the URL of the IdP server issuer endpoint. For example, if the domain is my.domain, the IdP application name is identityprovider, and the environment is configured for secure connections, then the value is: https://my.domain/identityprovider.
5.  For the key "ClientID", edit the value to specify the unique identifier of the IdP service that works with Desktop Host.
6.  For the key "Scope", edit the value to specify a list of access privileges requested by the client. Each scope name must be separated with a space. For example, openid offline_access.
7.  For the key "IdPServerTimeout", edit the value to specify a time in seconds after which the server becomes unavailable.  Note: By default, the value is 300 seconds. If you do not specify a value, this value is used.
8.  Save the config.json file.
9.  Do one of the following:
-
If you have not yet installed Desktop Host, place the config.json file in either the same folder as the Desktop Host installer (for Windows) or in the Home folder (for macOS).

-
If you have already installed Desktop Host, restart the computer.

## Configuring Desktop Host To Load All Services

Desktop Host is configured by default to load all services when the first connection is made by a client to Desktop Host. By default, the value for LoadServicesOnStartup is considered false to retain this existing behavior. If you need to load services on startup, you must add this configuration to the config.json file and set this to true. This section provides information on how to configure Desktop Host so that all services can be loaded when a user logs on to the system.

To configure Desktop Host to load all services:

1.  Open the config.json file in a plain-text editor with elevated privileges. In a typical installation, this file is in one of the following locations:
- After Windows installation, the file is in C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost\bin.
- After macOS installation, the file is in /Library/Hyland/DesktopHost/bin.
2.  In the file, add the following text after an existing configuration:
```
"LoadServicesOnStartup": true
```

For example:

```
{
   "WhitelistedAddress": [
        "https://www.example1.com",
        "https://www.example2.com"
   ],
   "LoadServicesOnStartup": true
}
```

Note: Ensure that each key value element in config.json is separated by a comma.
3.  Save the file and close the text editor.
4.  Restart the computer.

## Configuring Desktop Host To Load Specific Services

Desktop Host is configured by default to load all services that are available in the DesktopHost\services directory. You can configure specific services present in the DesktopHost\services  directory that are loaded when Desktop Host starts. This section provides information on how to configure Desktop Host so that specific services can be loaded when a user logs on to the system.

To configure Desktop Host to load specific services:

1.  Open the config.json file in a plain-text editor with elevated privileges. In a typical installation, this file is in one of the following locations:
- After Windows installation, the file is in C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost\bin.
- After macOS installation, the file is in /Library/Hyland/DesktopHost/bin.
2.  In the file, add the following text:
```
{
   "WhitelistedServiceFolders": []
}
```

3.  For the key "WhitelistedServiceFolders", replace the default value [ ] with the name of the folders present directly under the DesktopHost\services directory and containing the required services.
A folder name must be included within double quotation marks, and multiple folder names must be separated with commas. For example:

```
{
   "WhitelistedServiceFolders": ["Example_Folder_1","Example_Folder_ 2"]
}
```

Note: If no value is provided for the key "WhitelistedServiceFolders", all services present in the DesktopHost\services directory are loaded. Ensure that each key value element is separated by a comma.
4.  Save the file and close the text editor.
5.  Restart the computer.

## Configuring Document Classes

Each document that is brought into the system through a Content Queue configured for classification is assigned to a document class. Document classes group together documents with similar characteristics. A document assigned to a document class can be indexed using the fields associated with the document class. You can configure which fields are associated with a document class when you create a new document class or edit an existing document class.

-
For information on creating new document classes, see Creating Document Classes.

-
For information on editing existing document classes, see Editing Document Classes.

## Configuring Fields

Before creating a document class, you must create the fields associated with the document class. A field may be applied to several different document classes. When creating a field, you can configure the data type and format of the field.

-
For information on creating new fields, see Creating Fields.

-
For information on editing existing fields, see Editing Fields.

## Configuring Lists

You can assign a drop-down list with preconfigured values to fields associated with a document class. When creating a list, you can configure the data type and items to be contained in the list.

The list items are displayed in the Scan and Import and Verify Data interfaces based on the order they are arranged during configuration in the Administration interface. If a list is modified, the changes are available in the Scan and Import and Verify Data interfaces only when the batch is opened or captured. If a list is removed from a field, the list field reverts to its original field type.

## Configuring Log Files For Troubleshooting On Windows

Desktop Host is configured by default to send diagnostics logging messages to log files that can be used by Technical Support to diagnose and troubleshoot issues. This section provides information on how to configure Desktop Host in Windows to send additional information to the default log files and to modify the default logging configuration.

To configure Desktop Host logging in Windows:

1.  Open the config.json file in a plain-text editor with elevated privileges. In a typical installation, this file is in C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost\bin.
2.  In the file, locate the DesktopHostGateway_Log diagnostics route, within the Hyland.Logging element. By default, the route includes the following key and value elements, which you can edit as needed:
```
"DesktopHostGateway_Log":{
    "File":"%ProgramData%/Hyland/DesktopHost/logs/DesktopHostGateway_Log.json",
    "minimum-level":"Information",
    "FileRollInterval":"Day",
    "FileByteLimit":"10000000",
    "FileCountLimit":"30",
    "FileRollOnSize":"true",
    "OutputFormat":"Json",
    "include-profiles": "Desktop Host Gateway"
}
```

3.  For the key "File", replace the value "%ProgramData%/Hyland/DesktopHost/logs/DesktopHostGateway_Log.json" with the file path for the log file, including the name of the file you want the log to be saved as. This file must be a .json file.
For example, the default path mentioned above would write the logs to a file called DesktopHostGateway_Log`<date-stamp>`.json in the logs directory within the directory where Desktop Host is installed.

Note:
Ensure that the Windows user account running Desktop Host has write permission for the path specified in the "File" key.

4.   For the key "minimum-level", edit the value to the lowest level of severity you want to be reported in the log. The following log levels are available, listed from most severe to least severe:   Note:
Log level names are case sensitive.

| Log Level | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Critical | Logs that describe an unrecoverable application, system crash, or catastrophic failure that requires immediate attention. |
| Error | Logs that highlight when the current flow of execution is stopped due to a failure. These logs indicate a failure in the current activity, but not an application-wide failure. |
| Warning | Logs that highlight an abnormal or unexpected event in the application flow but do not otherwise cause the application to stop. |
| Information | Logs that track the general flow of the application. |
| Debug | Logs that are used for interactive investigation during development. |
| Trace | Logs that contain the most detailed messages and may include sensitive data. These logs should never be enabled in a production environment. |
| None | A logging category that does not write any logging messages. |

For example, the route could be edited to include the following attribute:

```
"minimum-level": "Information",
```

This example specifies that the logging route only receives logging messages with a severity level of Information or above.

Note:
The default severity level of a route is a minimum of Information and a maximum of Critical. The route uses these severity levels if it does not include a minimum-level line specified in the config.json file.

5.  For the key "FileRollInterval", edit the value to specify the interval after which you want a new log created. The following intervals are available for use:

| Interval | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Minute | A new log file will be created every minute. |
| Hour | A new log file will be created every hour. |
| Day | A new log file will be created every day. |
| Month | A new log file will be created every month. |
| Year | A new log file will be created every year. |
| Infinite | A new log file will never be created. |

6.  For the key "FileByteLimit", you can edit the value to specify the maximum size in bytes for a log file before a new file is created. This attribute is only active if the value for the key "FileRollOnSize" is set to "true".
7.  For the key "FileCountLimit", you can edit the value to specify the number of log files that are created before the oldest file is deleted. If you do not want to delete older files, you can set the value to "null".
8.  For the key "FileRollOnSize", you can edit the value to "true" if you want a new log file to be created when the current log file reaches the maximum size in bytes. If you do not want create new files based on file size, you can set the value to "false".  Note:
If "FileRollOnSize" is set to "true", the attribute "FileByteLimit" is active.

9.  For the key "OutputFormat", you can specify the structure of each message that would be written in the log file. The following formats are available for use:

| Format | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Minimal | Message that contains only the time, log level, exception, and the message field. |
| Text | Message that contains all possible fields as a list of key-value pairs. |
| Json | Message that contains fields in a compact JSON format on a single line. This format is ideal for further processing into a SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) or for reading with the Diagnostics Console. |

10.  In the file, locate the DesktopHostCore_Log diagnostics route, within the Hyland.Logging element. By default, the route includes the following key and value elements, which you can edit as needed:
```
"DesktopHostCore_Log":{
    "File":"%LocalAppData%/Hyland/DesktopHost/logs/DesktopHostCore_Log.json",
    "minimum-level":"Information",
    "FileRollInterval":"Day",
    "FileByteLimit":"10000000",
    "FileCountLimit":"30",
    "FileRollOnSize":"true",
    "OutputFormat":"Json",
    "include-profiles": "Desktop Host Core"
}
```

11.  For the key "File", replace the value "%LocalAppData%/Hyland/DesktopHost/logs/DesktopHostCore_Log.json" with the file path for the log file, including the name of the file you want the log to be saved as. This file must be a .json file. For example, the default path mentioned above would write the logs to a file called DesktopHostCore_Log`<date-stamp>`.json in the logs directory within the directory created for a user.Note: Ensure that the Windows user account running Desktop Host has write permission for the path specified in the "File" key.
12.  For the other key and value pairs in the diagnostic route DesktopHostCore_Log, edit those as needed.  Note: Ensure the key "include-profiles" includes the value "Desktop Host Core".
13.  In the file, locate the DesktopHostLauncher_Log diagnostics route, within the Hyland.Logging element. By default, the route includes the following key and value elements, which you can edit as needed:
```
"DesktopHostLauncher_Log":{
    "File":"%LocalAppData%/Hyland/DesktopHost/logs/DesktopHostLauncher_Log.json",
    "minimum-level":"Information",
    "FileRollInterval":"Day",
    "FileByteLimit":"10000000",
    "FileCountLimit":"30",
    "FileRollOnSize":"true",
    "OutputFormat":"Json",
    "include-profiles": "Desktop Host Launcher"
}
```

14.  For the key "File", replace the value "%LocalAppData%/Hyland/DesktopHost/logs/DesktopHostLauncher_Log.json" with the file path for the log file, including the name of the file you want the log to be saved as. This file must be a .json file. For example, the default path mentioned above would write the logs to a file called DesktopHostLauncher_Log`<date-stamp>`.json in the logs directory within the directory created for a user.Note: Ensure that the Windows user account running Desktop Host has write permission for the path specified in the "File" key.
15.  For the other key and value pairs in the diagnostic route DesktopHostLauncher_Log, edit those as needed.  Note: Ensure the key "include-profiles" includes the value "Desktop Host Launcher".
16.  Save the file and close the text editor.
17.  Restart the computer.

## Conventions

Review the following writing conventions to optimize your usage of the Capture REST API guide.

## Placeholders

This guide includes many different code examples to provide a starting point for developers to make requests to the Capture REST API and to understand responses from the server. Many of these examples include placeholder text in square brackets (for example, [base_url]), which represents values specific to the Hyland Capture user making the request or receiving the response. To make successful requests to the server, you must replace the placeholder text with values appropriate for your user account or resource (for example, always replace the [access_token] placeholder with the actual string representing the token you retrieved for your current session).

## Curl Commands

The tutorials in this guide describe how to make API calls using client URL (cURL, or curl) commands. Each curl command may contain many pieces of data, which can be difficult to read when written as one long line that requires horizontal scrolling. To improve readability, where feasible, this guide uses the \ character to mark artificial line breaks to visually separate different pieces of a curl command. Depending on the command line application you use to run the curl commands (for example, Command Prompt or Terminal), you may need to remove the line breaks or otherwise edit any copied curl examples to align with the syntax preferred by the application.

## Endpoints

To make calls to the Capture REST API, you must include the URL of your Hyland Capture environment directly before the endpoint in your request. In this guide's tutorials, this URL is represented by the [base_url] placeholder in sample requests (for example, GET [base_url]/api/export/batch). However, in this guide's reference topics, the [base_url] placeholder is omitted for simplicity (for example, GET /api/export/batch).

Additionally, this guide includes several different endpoints that are associated with similar or identical objects. Where feasible, the Endpoints sections refer to common objects in the Objects sections to reduce redundancies.

## Creating A Bar Code Profile

Creating a new bar code profile is a two-step process. In the first step, you can define the bar code properties and in the second step, you can define how the bar code is to be used. You can navigate to the second page, the Usage Definition screen only after you have entered all the mandatory information in the first page, the Define Bar Code Profile screen. After you have successfully entered the relevant details, the step number beside the screen name changes to a green tick.

To create a bar code profile:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Bar Code Profiles tab. The Bar Code Profiles screen is displayed.    ![The Bar code Profiles list screen](data:image/gif;base64,)    Note: The bar code profile list displays the code type and specific value assigned to each bar code profile. It also provdes information on whether a bar code profile is used for separation on one or more content queues, or classification on one or more document classes.
2.  Click the + Create Bar Code Profile button. The Define Bar Code Profile screen is displayed.   ![Define Bar Code Profile screen](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  In the Define Bar Code Profile screen, enter a Bar Code Profile Name for the bar code profile. This is how the bar code profile is represented in the application.
4.  Enter a Bar Code Profile Description.
5.  Uploading a sample document containing a bar code helps to configure the bar code profile settings and ensures better bar code detec tion, automatically generating the bar code type, value, and scanning area. If a sample document is unavailable, you must manually configure these settings.
-
To upload a sample document with a bar code, drag or browse to a sample file and configure the associated options (see Uploading a Sample Bar Code Document).

- To manually configure the bar code type, value, and scanning area, proceed to step 6.
6.  Select the bar code type from the Bar Code Type drop-down list.  Tip:
See Supported Bar Code Types for a list of all supported bar code types.

When selecting a bar code type, also note the following:

-
You must specify a bar code type for all separation methods.

-
The Patch 2 and Patch T types are only respected if the Separate on every occurrence separation method is selected.

7.  Enter a value or multiple values in the Bar Code Value field, if applicable and press Enter. You can also use a comma to separate multiple values and press Enter.  Note: This field is only displayed if the Separate on specific value method is selected. This field is deactivated and displayed as an empty field if you select Patch 2 or Patch T from the Bar Code Type list. If the bar code profile was not saved with Patch 2 or Patch T bar code types, on selection of a different bar code type, the last saved bar code value is again displayed.   Note: If the profile is assigned for separation and Separate on specific value is selected as the separation method, the profile separates based on any value matching the values in this field.    Note: If the profile is assigned for classification, a document must contain one of the specific values for the type and region selected to classify the document. If no specific values are specified, the document is classified based on the type and region alone from the settings input on step 1 and ignore the actual value of the detected bar codes.
8.  To specify the bar code position manually, enter values in the following fields in the Bar Code Area and Bar Code Area Size sections:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Starting Position From Left | Specifies the starting position of the bar code area as measured from the upper-left corner of the page. This percentage value ranges from 0 to 99. |
| Starting Position From Top | Specifies the starting position of the bar code area as measured from the top of the page. This percentage value ranges from 0 to 99. |
| Width | Specifies the width of the bar code area with respect to the page. This percentage value ranges from 1 to 100. |
| Height | Specifies the height of the bar code area with respect to the page. This percentage value ranges from 1 to 100. |

9.  Click the Next button. The Usage Definition screen is displayed.   ![Usage Definition screen](data:image/gif;base64,)    Note: The Next button is activated only when all the relevant information has been filled in all the mandatory fields.
10.  In the Usage Configuration screen, in the Separation Options section, from the Select Separation Method drop-down list select one of the following options:

-

-

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Separate on every occurrence | Separates the document every time a specific type of bar code is detected. This is the default separation method. |
| Separate on change | Separates the document when the bar code value changes. This method does not consider a specific bar code value; it separates the document only when a change in bar code values is detected. When selecting the Separate on change method, also note the following:   The first detected bar code of the specified type is considered a change and triggers a split, even when no prior value has been detected.   Since only one value can be used for processing, the Separate on change method does not separate the document if it detects multiple bar codes that match the acceptance criteria. |
| Separate on specific value | Separates the document when a bar code of a specific value is detected. When this method is selected, the Bar Code Value field is displayed on the Create Separation Profile screen. Enter the specific bar code value in the field. |

11.  Select one of the following options in the Select page for separation section:
| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Bar code identifies the first page of the document | The bar code used for separation identifies the first page of the document. |
| Bar code identifies the last page of the document | The bar code used for separation identifies the last page of the document. |

12.  To delete the page containing the bar code that is used for document separation, select the Delete the page which is containing the barcode used to separate a document check box.
13.  The Assigned Content Queues list, displays all the content queues assigned to this bar code profile.
14.  To delete the page containing the bar code that is used for document classification, in the Classification Options section, select the Delete the page which is containing the barcode used to classify a document check box.
15.  The Assigned Classes list, displays all the classes assigned to this bar code profile.
16.  Click Save.  Note: To go to the Define Bar Code Profile screen, click the Back button.

## Creating A List

You can configure lists and assign them to a field. A field with a list assigned appears as a drop-down list in the Scan and Import and Verifier intefaces.

To create a list:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Lists tab.
2.  Click + Create New List or Create New List. The Create new list screen is displayed.   Note: Create New List appears only when no lists have been created previously.    ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Enter a Display Name for the list.
4.  Enter a Description of the list.
5.  Select the data type of the list from the Data Type drop-down list. By default, the Alphanumeric data type is selected.
6.  In the Add List Items box, enter the items that you want to include in this list. You can add maximum of 100 items and each list item can be of a maximum of 50 characters.
- To filter the list items displayed, in the Search field, enter a string of characters contained in the name of the list you want to select.
- To change how the list items are organized, select the appropriate option from the Sort By drop-down list.The list items appear in the list in the same order in which they were added.
Note: The value format of the list items must match the specified data type of the list.
7.  Click Save.

## Creating Content Queues

To create a Content Queue:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Content Queues tab.
2.  Click + Create Content Queue. The Create Content Queue configuration options are displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Enter a Display Name for the Content Queue. This is how the Content Queue is represented in the Scan and Import and Verify Data interfaces. You may enter a Description for the Content Queue as well.
4.  To assign a user group to the Content Queue, from the Assign user groups list, select the relevant user groups.  Note: Users can view and work only with the Content Queues to which they are assigned. You can assign multiple user groups to a Content Queue. After the assignment, all the users in the assigned groups can work with the Content Queue. If no groups are assigned, the Content Queue is not visible to anyone.
5.  Select the Batch Verification process.
-
If documents are to be manually classified and indexed without verification, select Verify at Scan Time. If at least one document class assigned to the Content Queue has fields assigned to it, users are able to classify and index documents in the Scan and Import interface. If no document class in a Verify at Scan Time Content Queue has fields assigned to it, or if a Verify at Scan Time Content Queue has no document classes associated with it, users are only able to classify documents in the Scan and Import interface.

-
If users are to manually classify and index documents, and the documents are to undergo verification, select Verify from Batch List. Users may classify documents in either the Scan and Import or Verify Data interfaces, and users index documents in the Verify Data interface.

Tip:
A Verify at Scan Time Content Queue is well-suited for scenarios in which indexing is to be completed quickly by the same user who acquired the documents. A Verify from Batch List Content Queue can be used in scenarios in which indexing is to be completed later in the process, potentially by a different user.

6.  Configure the options specific to each process flow type:
-
If you selected Verify at Scan Time, and users are to be able to search text on exported PDFs, select Include searchable text on exported PDF.

-
If you selected If you selected Verify from Batch List, continue to step 10.

7.  To add the appropriate bar code separation profiles to the Content Queue, click Add Profile. The Add Bar Code Separation Profile dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
8.  Select one or more bar code separation profiles to add to the Content Queue and click Add X Profiles, where X is the number of profiles you selected. The profiles are added to the Content Queue and the Add Bar Code Separation Profile dialog box is closed.  Note:
The same bar code separation profile can be added to multiple Content Queues.

9.  To reorder the bar code separation profiles associated with the Content Queue, click the dots icon to the left of the profile you want to move and drag the profile to a new position in the list of profiles.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The higher a profile is positioned in the list on the Content Queue, the greater priority is given to the profile. This priority determines which profile to use for page separation when multiple profiles are detected on a single page during a batch capture process.

10.  To add the appropriate document classes to the Content Queue, click Add Class. The Add Classes dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
11.  Select one or more document classes to add to the Content Queue and click Add X Classes, where X is the number of document classes you selected. The document classes are added to the Content Queue and the Add Classes dialog box is closed.
12.  To reorder the classes associated with the Content Queue, click the dots icon to the left of the class you want to move and drag the class to a new position in the list of classes.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The higher a class is positioned in the list on the Content Queue, the greater priority is given to the class. This priority determines the order of class assignment during bar code classification when multiple classes are detected for a single document.

13.  To view the class details, click the class name.  Note: To modify the class details in the Classes page, click Go to class.
14.  To configure which fields on a document class are required for indexing:
1.  Click the Field Options button of the corresponding document class. The field options dialog box for the corresponding document class is displayed.
2.  For all fields that are required, select the Required option.
3.  Click OK. The field options dialog box is closed.
15.  Click Okay.
16.  Click Save.

## Creating Document Classes

To create a document class:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Classes tab.
2.  Click + Create Class. The Create Document Class screen is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Enter a Display Name for the document class. You may enter a Description for the document class as well.  Note:
The Description is only displayed when viewing the document class in the Administration user interface.

4.  To add appropriate bar code profiles to the document class, click Add Profile. The Add Bar Code Classification Profile dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Select the bar code profiles to add to the document class.
6.  Click Add Profile after selecting the appropriate bar code profiles. The bar code profiles are added to the document class.
7.  To reorder the bar code profiles associated with the class, click the dots icon to the left of the bar code profile you want to move and drag the bar code profile to a new position in the list of bar code profiles.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The higher a bar code profile is positioned in the list, the greater priority is given to it. This priority determines the order of bar code profiles during the indexing of document class when multiple bar code profiles are detected for a single document class.

8.  To add the appropriate fields to the document class, click Add Fields. The Add Fields dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
9.  Select the fields to add to the document class. To select all fields displayed, select the Field option at the top of the list.
10.  Click Add Field after selecting the appropriate fields. The fields are added to the document class.
11.  To reorder the fields associated with the class, click the dots icon to the left of the fields you want to move and drag the field to a new position in the list of fields.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The higher a field is positioned in the list, the greater priority is given to it. This priority determines the order of fields during the indexing of document class when multiple fields are detected for a single document class.

12.  To configure bar code extraction from a field, click Configure Field. The Field: `<Name>` dialog box is displayed.  Note: A field must be saved to a document class before it can be configured for bar code extraction. The Configure Field button is available only when the field is saved.
13.  From the Assign Extraction Profile drop-down list, select the bar code profile to be assigned to the field for extraction. To know more about the bar code extraction arbitration logic, see Bar Code Extraction Arbitration Logic.  Note: Bar code profiles with patch code as the bar code type cannot be assigned.
14.  Click Save.  After a profile is assigned, a bar code icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) next to the field name indicates that bar code extraction has been configured for that field.

## Creating Fields

To create a field:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Fields tab.
2.  Click + Create Field. The Create Field screen is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Enter a Display Name for the field. You may enter a Description for the field as well.
4.  Select the data type of the field from the Data Type drop-down list.  Note: You cannot change the Data Type after the field is saved and created.
5.  The Formatting list displays the format of the field.
6.  To assign a list to this field, select the Assign list values check box. If you clear the Assign list values check box, no list remains associated with the field and you need to reassign a list from the Assign list values check box.
7.  Select the list from the Available lists drop-down list. The lists availabledepend on the data type of the field.  Note: To view more details about the liste items of the selected list, select the View Details button.
8.  Click Save.

## Deleting An Input Source

You can delete an existing input source if you no longer need it for batch capture processing.

To delete an input source:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, submit or delete the batch (see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export). The document capture layout is displayed.
3.  In the Input Source pane on the right side of the layout, click the vertical ellipsis icon to the right of the input source you want to delete.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down list is expanded, displaying options for changing the input source.

4.  Select Delete. A confirmation message is displayed.
5.  Click Delete to confirm.

## Editing A Bar Code Profile

To edit a bar code profile:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Bar Code Profiles tab.
2.  Click the bar code profile to be edited. The Define Bar Code Profile screen is displayed.   ![Define Bar Code Profile screen](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Edit the settings available here for the bar code profile. For more information on these settings, see Creating a Bar Code Profile.
4.  Click the Next button to navigate to the Usage Configuration screen. The configurable settings for the profile are displayed.    ![Usage Configuration screen](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Edit the settings available here for the bar code profile. For more information on these settings, see Creating a Bar Code Profile.
6.  Click Save.

## Editing A List

The modifications made to the lists in the Administration interface are available in the Scan and Import and Verifier interfaces only when the batch with the updated list field is re-opened.

To edit a list:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Lists tab.
2.  Select the list to be edited. The configurable options for the list are displayed.    ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Edit the Display Name for the list.
4.  Edit the Description of the list.
5.  You cannot edit the Data Type of a list after it is saved.
6.  In the Add List Items box, edit the list items.
- To filter the list items displayed, in the Search field, enter a string of characters contained in the name of the list you want to select.
- To change how the list items are organized, select the appropriate option from the Sort By drop-down list.The list items appear in the list in the same order in which they were added.Note: The value format of the list items must match the specified data type of the list.
7.  Click Save.

## Editing An Input Source

You can edit the settings of an existing input source if you want to retain some of its settings but adjust others to better meet your batch capture processing needs.

To edit an input source:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, submit or delete the batch (see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export). The document capture layout is displayed.
3.  In the Input Source pane on the right side of the layout, click the vertical ellipsis icon to the right of the input source you want to edit.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down list is expanded, displaying options for changing the input source.

4.  Select Edit. The Edit Input Source page is displayed.
5.  Edit the settings available for the input source. For more information on these settings, see the following sections:
-
Adding a Sweep Input Source

-
Adding a Scan Input Source

Note:
If you need to modify the hardware settings of your scanner while editing a scan input source, you must click Open Hardware Settings to access the scanner settings dialog box. See the scanner's documentation for more information on the available scanner settings.

6.  Click Save.

## Editing Content Queues

To edit a Content Queue:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Content Queue tab.
2.  Select the Content Queue you wish to edit from the filter list.
-
To filter the list of Content Queues displayed, enter in the Search field a string of characters contained in the name of the Content Queue you want to select.

-
To change how the list is organized, select the appropriate option from the Sort By drop-down list.

The configurable options for the Content Queue are displayed.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Edit the Display Name for the Content Queue.
4.  To modify the user group assignment, from the Assign user groups list, select the relevant user groups.  Note: Users can view and work only with the Content Queues to which they are assigned. You can assign multiple user groups to a Content Queue. After the assignment, all the users in the assigned groups can work with the Content Queue. If no groups are assigned, the Content Queue is not visible to anyone.
5.  Select the Batch Verification  process.
-
If documents are to be manually classified and indexed without verification, select Verify at Scan Time. If at least one document class assigned to the Content Queue has fields assigned to it, users are able to classify and index documents in the Scan and Import interface. If no document class in the content Queue has fields assigned to it, or if the Content Queue has no document classes associated with it, users are only able to classify documents.

-
If users are to manually classify and index documents, and the documents are to undergo verification, select Verify from Batch List. Users may classify documents in either the Scan and Import or Verify Data interfaces, and users index documents in the Verify Data interface.

Tip:
A Verify at Scan Time Content Queue is well-suited for scenarios in which indexing is to be completed quickly by the same user who acquired the documents. A Verify from Batch List Content Queue can be used in scenarios in which indexing is to be completed later in the process, potentially by a different user.

6.  Configure the options specific to each process flow type:
-
If you selected Verify at Scan Time, and users are to be able to search text on exported PDFs, select Include searchable text on exported PDF.

-
If you selected If you selected Verify from Batch List, continue to step 10.

7.  To add the appropriate bar code separation profiles to the Content Queue, click Add Profile. The Add Bar Code Separation Profile dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
8.  Select one or more bar code separation profiles to add to the Content Queue and click Add X Profiles, where X is the number of profiles you selected. The profiles are added to the Content Queue and the Add Bar Code Separation Profile dialog box is closed.  Note:
The same bar code separation profile can be added to multiple Content Queues.

9.  To reorder the bar code separation profiles associated with the Content Queue, click the dots icon to the left of the profile you want to move and drag the profile to a new position in the list of profiles.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The higher a profile is positioned in the list on the Content Queue, the greater priority is given to the profile. This priority determines which profile to use for page separation when multiple profiles are detected on a single page during a batch capture process.

10.  To add the appropriate document classes to the Content Queue, click Add Class. The Add Classes dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
11.  Select one or more document classes to add to the Content Queue and click Add X Classes, where X is the number of document classes you selected. The document classes are added to the Content Queue and the Add Classes dialog box is closed.
12.  To configure which fields on a document class are required by the Content Queue:
1.  Click the Field Options button of the corresponding document class. The field options dialog box for the corresponding document class are displayed.
2.  For all fields that are required, select the Required option.
3.  Click OK. The field options dialog box is closed.
13.  To edit a document class associated with the Content Queue:
1.  Click the class name.
2.  In the Class: <Class Name> dialog box, click Go to Class. You are redirected to the Classes tab, and the configuration options for the corresponding document class are displayed.
3.  Edit the document class and click Save. For more information, see Editing Document Classes.
14.  To remove a document class from the Content Queue:
1.  Click the ![](data:image/gif;base64,) icon corresponding to the document class you want to delete.
2.  Click Remove Class. A confirmation message is displayed.
3.  Click Remove to remove the document class from the Content Queue.
15.  To make changes to a bar code separation profile associated with the Content Queue, you can edit the profile from the Bar Code Separation Options section of the Content Queue's configuration options.  Note:
Editing a bar code separation profile affects that profile on any Content Queue the profile is associated with, regardless of which Content Queue is selected when you begin editing.

To edit a bar code separation profile associated with the Content Queue:

1.  Click the bar code profile name.
2.  In the Bar Code Profile: <Name> dialog box, click Go to profile. You are redirected to the Bar Code Profiles tab, and the configuration options for the corresponding bar code profile are displayed.
3.  Edit the bar code separation profile and click Save. For more information, see Editing a Bar Code Profile.
16.  To remove a bar code profile from the Content Queue:
1.  Click the ![](data:image/gif;base64,) icon corresponding to the bar code profile you want to delete.
2.  Click Remove Profile. A confirmation message is displayed.
3.  Click Remove to remove the bar code separation profile from the Content Queue.  Note:
Removing a bar code separation profile from a Content Queue does not delete the profile from the Bar Code Profiles tab or affect batches that have already been captured when the profile was associated with the Content Queue.

17.  Click Okay.
18.  Click Save.

## Editing Document Classes

To edit a document class:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Classes tab.
2.  Select the document class to be edited.
-
To filter the list of document classes displayed, enter in the Search field a string of characters contained in the name of the document class you want to select.

-
To change how the list is organized, select the appropriate option from the Sort By drop-down list.

The configurable settings for the document class are displayed.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Edit the Display Name for the document class. You may edit the Description for the document class as well.  Note: The Description is only displayed when viewing the document class in the Administration interface.
4.  To add additional bar code profiles to the document class, click Add Profile. The Add Bar Code Classification Profile dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Select the bar code profiles to add to the document class.
6.  Click Add Profile after selecting the appropriate bar code profiles. The bar code profiles are added to the document class.
7.  To edit a bar code profile associated with the document class:
1.   Click the bar code profile name.
2.  In the Bar Code Profile: <Profile Name> dialog box, click Go To profile. You are redirected to the Bar Code Profiles tab, and the configuration options for the corresponding bar code profile are displayed.
3.  Edit the profile and click Save. For more information, see Editing Bar Code Profiles.
8.  To remove a bar code profile from the document class:
1.  Click the delete icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) corresponding to the bar code profile you want to delete.
2.  Click Remove to remove the bar code profile from the document class.
9.  To reposition a bar code profile assigned to the document class, click to the left of the name of the bar code profile and drag the field to a new position.
10.  To add additional fields to the document class, click Add Fields. The Add Fields dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
11.  Select the fields to add to the document class. To select all fields displayed, select the Field option at the top of the list.
12.  Click Add Field after selecting the appropriate fields. The fields are added to the document class.
13.  To edit a field associated with the document class:
1.   Click the field name.
2.  In the Field: <Field Name> dialog box, click Go to Field. You are redirected to the Fields tab, and the configuration options for the corresponding field are displayed.
3.  Edit the field and click Save. For more information, see Editing Fields.
14.  To remove a field from the document class:
1.  Click the delete icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) corresponding to the field profile you want to delete.
2.  Click Remove to remove the field from the document class.
15.  To reposition a field assigned to the document class, click to the left of the name of the field and drag the field to a new position.
16.  To edit the bar code extraction configuration:
1.  Click Configure Field beside the appropriate field.
1.  In the Field: <Name> dialog box, from the Assign Extraction Profile drop-down list, select the bar code profile to be assigned to the field for extraction.
1.  To remove a bar code extraction configuration, in the Field: <Name> dialog box, from the Assign Extraction Profile drop-down list, select No Profile Assigned.
17.  Click Save.

## Editing Fields

To edit a field:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Fields tab.
2.  Select the field to be edited.
-
To filter the list of fields displayed, enter in the Search field a string of characters contained in the name of the field you want to select.

-
To change how the list is organized, select the appropriate option from the Sort By drop-down list.

The configurable options for the field are displayed.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Edit the Display Name for the field.  Note: After a field is created and saved, you cannot modify its Data Type.
4.  The Formatting list displays the format of the field.
5.  To assign a list to this field, select the Assign list values check box.
6.  Select a list from the Available lists drop-down list.
7.  Click Save.

## Issue With Delay While Starting Desktop Host

Issue: While Desktop Host is started for the first time or a user logs on to a computer, Desktop Host faces an unexpected delay before starting.

Solution: Desktop Host is configured by default to load all services that are available in the DesktopHost\services directory. You can configure specific services present in the DesktopHost\services directory that are loaded when Desktop Host starts.

To configure Desktop Host to load specific services:

1.  Open the config.json file in a plain-text editor with elevated privileges. In a typical installation, this file is in one of the following locations:
- After Windows installation, the file is in C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost\bin.
- After macOS installation, the file is in /Library/Hyland/DesktopHost/bin.
2.  In the file, add the following text:
```
{
   "WhitelistedServiceFolders": []
}
```

3.  For the key "WhitelistedServiceFolders", replace the default value [ ] with the name of the folders present directly under the DesktopHost\services directory and containing the required services.
A folder name must be included within double quotation marks, and multiple folder names must be separated with commas. For example:

```
{
   "WhitelistedServiceFolders": ["Example_Folder_1","Example_Folder_ 2"]
}
```

Note: If no value is provided for the key "WhitelistedServiceFolders", all services present in the DesktopHost\services directory are loaded. Ensure that each key value element is separated by a comma.
4.  Save the file and close the text editor.
5.  Restart the computer.

## Opening The Administration Interface

While working in the Hyland Experience Capture application, you can switch to the Administration interface from the Scan and Import or Verify Data interfaces as needed. You can also open the Hyland Experience Capture Administration interface from the Hyland Experience Administration Portal.

Note: You need to have a valid subscription to access the application.
To switch to the Administration interface:

1.  Open the Hyland Experience Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). Alternatively, navigate to the Hyland Experience Administration Portal.
2.  Open the Administration interface.
-
If you opened the Hyland Experience Capture application, click the Administration Configuration button in the upper-right corner of the application.

-
If you want to access the Administration interface from the Hyland Experience Administration Portal, select the appropriate environment, and click Hyland Experience Capture Config.

3.  The Administration interface is displayed in a new tab.

## Splitting And Creating A Document With Selected Pages

You can select a number of pages and split a document to create a new document with the selected pages.

To split selected pages of a document:

1.  On the relevant document area, do one of the following:
-
To select a single page, click the thumbnail image of the page from which you want to create a new document and select the check box in the upper-left corner.

-
To select multiple pages, click the thumbnail image and select the check box in the upper-left corner of each thumbnail image. A tick appears on the selected pages.

2.  In the upper-right corner of the document area, click the vertical ellipsis icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
A drop-down menu is displayed.

3.  Click Split selected pages or press Alt + L. A new document is created with the selected pages.

## Troubleshooting Desktop Host

This section describes common issues you may encounter with Desktop Host and how to resolve them.

See the following topics for details:

-
Chrome Restricts Desktop Host

-
Error During Installation on Windows

-
Issue with Executing Commands and Streaming

-
Issue with Delay While Starting Desktop Host

-
Issue with Uninstalling or Repairing Desktop Host

## Whitelisting A Domain

Desktop Host can communicate only with domains that are included in a preconfigured whitelist. You can edit the config.json file included in the Desktop Host installation. This file is delivered with the installer for Desktop Host. When you run the installer, the file is copied to the ..\bin directory of the installed application. You can edit the file before or after installation. If you edit the file after installation, you must restart the computer to honor the new list of domains.

To add or modify domains to a whitelist:

1.  Open the config.json file in a plain-text editor. In a typical installation, this file is in one of the following locations:
-
Before installation, the file is in the same directory as the installer.

-
After Windows installation, the file is in C:\ProgramData\Hyland\DesktopHost\bin.

-
After macOS installation, the file is in /Library/Hyland/DesktopHost/bin.

2.  In the WhitelistedAddress section, enter the domains that you want to whitelist. Each domain must be in double quotation marks, and multiple domains must be separated with commas. For example:
```
{
    "WhitelistedAddress": [
        "https://www.example.com",
        "https://www.example2.com"
    ]
}
```

Only include the domains for addresses that you want to whitelist; do not include full URL addresses. For example, to communicate with https://www.example.com/public/, include only https://example.com on the whitelist.   Note:
By default, the whitelist contains the domain 127.0.0.1:8080. You may remove or modify this domain if you do not want to allow communication at that address.

3.  Save the config.json file.
4.  Do one of the following:
-
If you have not yet installed Desktop Host, place the config.json file in either the same folder as the Desktop Host installer (for Windows) or in the Home folder (for macOS).

-
If you have already installed Desktop Host, restart the computer.

## Working With Input Sources

While Content Queues and document classes are pre-configured by your system administrator, you can manage your own input sources to better tailor them to your batch capture processes.

See the following sections for details:

-
Adding an Input Source

-
Editing an Input Source

-
Deleting an Input Source
